text: Day 53 roundup: CEASEFIRE EXPIRY EVE. Iran refuses Islamabad Round 2 — Baqaei: 'no plans.' VP Aref: US demands 'childish.' BUT senior official: 'positively reviewing.' Vance delegation departed for Pakistan anyway. Araghchi-Lavrov call — Russia condemns blockade. Brent $96.26 (+20.6%). Gas $4.04. Iran death toll 3,375. Ceasefire expires TOMORROW.
verificationNote: Compiled from Axios, Al Jazeera, BusinessToday, Washington Times, CNBC, AAA.

text: CENTCOM blockade Day 8: 27 vessels redirected since April 13. Touska (900-ft Iranian cargo ship) towed to Fujairah after USS Spruance fired on engine room. Iran Hormuz blockade Day 50 — effectively closed. Zero tanker transits. 20,000+ seafarers stranded. Iran approaching floating storage deadline April 26.
verificationNote: CNBC, NPR, Al Jazeera maritime tracking confirmed.

text: DEATH TOLL UPDATE: Iran forensic chief confirms 3,375 dead (2,875 men, 496 women, 383 children) from Feb 28 to April 20. Washington Times confirms. Health Ministry official count: 2,740. HRANA count: 3,636. Lebanon: 2,387 killed, 7,602 wounded per WAFA. Total cross-conflict dead exceeds 5,100.
verificationNote: Washington Times, WAFA, HRANA reporting confirmed.

text: DIPLOMATIC COLLAPSE: Iran FM spokesman Baqaei — 'no plans for next round of negotiations.' VP Aref calls US demands 'childish.' Trump: ceasefire extension 'highly unlikely,' threatens every power plant and bridge. But Vance delegation departed for Pakistan anyway. Senior Iranian official: 'positively reviewing.' Contradictory signals to the last.
verificationNote: BusinessToday, CNN, Al Jazeera confirmed all quotes.

text: RUSSIA BACKING: Araghchi-Lavrov phone call April 21. Russia condemns US naval blockade as 'unlawful under international law.' Russian oil sanctions waiver extended through mid-May. Lavrov reiterates support for Iran's sovereignty. Moscow increasingly vocal in supporting Tehran's position.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera live updates, Fortune confirmed waiver extension.

text: OIL MARKETS: Brent $96.26 (+20.6% from pre-war), WTI $86.27 (+14.3%). Markets pricing in high probability of hostilities resuming after ceasefire expiry. Iran blockade costing $400-435M/day. Gold $4,804/oz. S&P 7,109. Iran approaching floating storage capacity deadline April 26.
verificationNote: CNBC, PBS, TradingEconomics, AAA confirmed prices.

text: CEASEFIRE EXPIRY COUNTDOWN: Less than 24 hours until April 22 expiry. Key factors: Iran has refused talks. Trump threatened total infrastructure destruction. Vance en route Pakistan (Iran may not show). CENTCOM blockade expanding. IRGC rearmed during ceasefire (Gen. Naqdi: missiles 'production date May 2026'). All signs point to resumption of hostilities.
verificationNote: Compiled from multiple sources. All claims independently verified.
